headline: Notre Dame's Ground Game Overwhelms Arkansas in Week Two Rout
body: Notre Dame's offense operated with surgical precision against Arkansas on Saturday, with quarterback Carr managing the game efficiently while the running back committee controlled the line of scrimmage. Smothers claimed Player of the Game honors by punching in two touchdowns on 14 carries, but the real story was Notre Dame's refusal to surrender the ball or the initiative. Arkansas managed only 310 total yards and never threatened seriously, with Jackson completing just 13 of 30 passes despite Clay's productive receiving work.

The decisive sequence came in the second half when Notre Dame's defense, anchored by Lucas's seven tackles, shut down Arkansas's already limited running game. Hodges managed just 25 yards on four attempts, forcing Jackson into obvious passing situations where the Notre Dame secondary tightened coverage. Fuller's touchdown grab stretched the lead to a point where Arkansas had no realistic path back into the game.

Notre Dame's balanced attack—333 passing yards, 145 rushing—demonstrated patience and field awareness that Arkansas simply could not match. The Irish committed no turnovers and allowed no sacks, establishing the kind of clean, methodical performance that compounds defensive pressure over four quarters. Arkansas kept the ball clean but generated no rhythm, settling into a rhythm of three-and-outs that depleted its defense.
turningPoint: Fuller's one-yard touchdown reception in the third quarter, which extended Notre Dame's lead to 28-10 and effectively ended Arkansas's comeback math.

unsungHero.name: Lucas
unsungHero.why: Seven tackles and a game-long presence that organized Notre Dame's defense while Smothers got the touchdown honors; Lucas's work in coverage and run support enabled the dominant second-half shutdown.
